column1: # Quest 5 corpus regression manifest: <game-basename> TAB <walkthrough-file> TAB <mode>
#   hints             -> Q&A/prose only, no linear command script; runner skips
# walkthrough-file "-": override-only row — no source walkthrough exists at all;
#   the game is driven purely by its overrides/<Game>.cmd (derived from source).
# optional 4th column (preamble): ";"-separated commands prepended before the
#   walkthrough, for a game whose walkthrough assumes a title-screen menu was
#   already dismissed (e.g. Dream Pieces needs "new game" or every command fails).
# The 25 games with a walkthrough (of 50), plus Dracula (walkthrough sourced
# externally from CASA solutionarchive — for the ORIGINAL 1986 CRL game, not
# this 2014 remake, hence a heavily-adapted override), The Brutal Murder of
# Jenny Lee (PDF-only walkthrough) and the two override-only wt="-" rows
# (The Shack, The Tree) = 28 driven rows. Filename mismatches vs the
# .quest names (Guttersnipe spacing, Jacqueline, Hawk suffix, bare .txt, The
# Deer Trail's walkthrough named after its working title "The Deer Blind") are
# why a naive "<Game> - walkthrough.txt" match silently misses several — hence
# The six formerly hints-only games (Q&A/prose walkthroughs with no linear
# command list) are driven by hand-derived winning scripts in overrides/.
